fact: In the Northern Hemisphere, this date falls during the darkest two weeks of the entire year - you're living through some of the gloomiest days possible!
Reindeer in the Arctic are now living in complete darkness 24 hours a day, relying on their thick winter coats and super-sensitive noses to find food in the snow.
The winter solstice is just 4 days away - the sun will reach its lowest point and barely peek over the horizon in northern places.
Penguins in Antarctica are experiencing their longest, brightest days right now, with some areas getting sunlight for 20+ hours straight!
On this date, the constellation Orion the Hunter reaches its highest point in the night sky around midnight, making his belt stars super easy to spot.
December 17 falls during the peak migration time for snowy owls, who swoop down from the Arctic to hunt in fields across North America and Europe.
Tree sap in cold regions has turned into nature's antifreeze by now, becoming so sugary and thick that it won't freeze even in sub-zero temperatures.
The sun sets before 5 PM across most of the northern United States on December 17, making afternoon feel like nighttime.
December 17 sits in the zodiac sign Sagittarius, represented by a centaur archer - half human, half horse, aiming arrows at the stars.
Polar bears are at their fluffiest right now, with their winter coats reaching maximum thickness to prepare for the coldest months ahead on the Arctic ice.
